steps.title: Identify the goal
steps.content: The question asks for the choice that best emphasizes a similarity between the two specimens. Both Stan (a Tyrannosaurus) and Horridus (a Triceratops) share exactly one detail in the notes: both lived in the Late Cretaceous period. A correct answer must name both specimens and highlight that shared time period.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — Both the *Tyrannosaurus* fossil specimen Stan and the *Triceratops* fossil specimen Horridus lived in the Late Cretaceous period.: Names both specimens and directly states their shared trait — the Late Cretaceous period. This is the similarity the question asks for. ✓
  • Choice B — While the *Tyrannosaurus* fossil specimen Stan was prepared in Hill City, South Dakota, the *Triceratops* fossil specimen Horridus is housed in Melbourne, Australia.: Highlights a difference, not a similarity. ✗
  • Choice C — Dinosaur fossil specimens can be found at science museums and research institutions all over the world, including the Melbourne Museum in Australia.: Makes a general claim about fossil specimens and does not compare Stan and Horridus directly. ✗
  • Choice D — Stan is a *Tyrannosaurus* fossil specimen that was prepared by the Black Hills Institute and lived in the Late Cretaceous period.: Mentions only one specimen and says nothing about Horridus. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
